commit.message:
docs: refresh README capabilities and add prerequisites section

Rewrite Key Capabilities in both README.md and README.ru.md to
emphasize user-facing value over internal architecture:

- Synchronized streaming via the Sendspin protocol with lossless audio
- No-console setup: scan, pair, and configure entirely from the web UI
- Deep Music Assistant integration (now playing, transport, groups, WebSocket)
- Home Assistant automations: BT speakers as MA player entities for
  scripts, scenes, dashboards, and voice assistants
- Multi-room ready with multi-bridge support
- Five deployment options with concrete 60+ endpoint count

Add a 'What you need' / 'Что потребуется' prerequisites section
before Quick Start in both READMEs.

Add ROADMAP.md link to Project links in both files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: show ball emojis in break history in /history set results

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add set-level pagination to /history

- build_history_embed now accepts set_page; shows all-set summary with
  a ◀ marker on the active set, and only that set's event log
- Row 0: ◀ Newer / Older ▶ (session navigation, resets set to 0)
- Row 1: ◀ Prev Set / Next Set ▶ (shown only when session has >1 set)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: group break history by player in /history set results

Each player now shows one line with all break totals joined by arrows:
  Tung: (1) -> (5) -> (1) -> (9)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: omit all end_turn lines from event log display

Ball and foul events provide enough context; turn boundaries are implicit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: restore missing setup() function in cogs/snooker.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: hide skipped turns (no balls potted) from event log display

In _format_events_grouped, end_turn lines are now only emitted when the
immediately preceding event is a ball event for the same player. Turns
where the player passed without scoring are silently omitted.

Applies to both the live scoreboard feed (/snooker) and /history logs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add /sync command to trigger Gitea mirror sync from Discord

- /sync calls POST /api/v1/repos/{repo}/mirror-sync on the Gitea instance
- GITEA_TOKEN, GITEA_URL, GITEA_MIRROR_REPO added to config.py (env vars)
- GITEA_TOKEN injected via discord-snooker-secret in deploy.yaml
- aiohttp added explicitly to requirements.txt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: guard against 6000-char total embed limit in /history

Add _embed_len helper and _safe_add_field wrapper that checks cumulative
embed size before every add_field call. Set Results chunks and event log
fields are skipped/noted when the 5900-char safety threshold is reached.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent history embed 400 error when Set Results exceeds 1024 chars

Replace single add_field call with a chunking loop that splits set_lines
into multiple 'Set Results (cont.)' fields, each within Discord's 1024-char
field value limit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: remove explicit kubeconfig credential, rely on python-ci agent auth

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: switch to kaniko build and helm container for deploy

- agent: python-ci pod template (kaniko + helm containers)
- Build & Push: kaniko executor replaces docker build/push; registry
  auth written to /kaniko/.docker/config.json from Jenkins credential
- Deploy: kubectl apply via helm container; removed docker post-cleanup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add RDS stack with PostgreSQL 17

- Add cdk_rds_stack.py: PostgreSQL 17 on db.t3.micro, default VPC
- Credentials auto-generated and stored in Secrets Manager
- CfnOutputs for DbEndpoint, DbPort, DbSecretArn
- RemovalPolicy.DESTROY and deletion_protection=False for dev
- Register CdkRdsStack and CdkLambdaStack in app.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add WinPython support to the System Python manager (#1369)

fixes:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-python-environments/issues/1251

PET can discover WinPython environments, but the extension was silently
dropping them because `WinPython` was not a recognized
`NativePythonEnvironmentKind`. WinPython is a portable Windows Python
distribution that belongs under the existing System Python manager
alongside `WindowsRegistry`, `WindowsStore`, and `GlobalPaths`.

## Changes

- **`nativePythonFinder.ts`** — Add `winpython = 'WinPython'` to the
`NativePythonEnvironmentKind` enum to match the value emitted by PET's
`pet-winpython` crate.
- **`managers/builtin/utils.ts`** — Add
`NativePythonEnvironmentKind.winpython` to the `refreshPythons`
allowlist so WinPython environments surface through the System Python
manager.

WinPython falls through to the `default` branch in `getKindName()`, so
it displays as `Python <version>` — consistent with other undecorated
system Pythons.

> [!WARNING]
>
> <details>
> <summary>Firewall rules blocked me from connecting to one or more
addresses (expand for details)</summary>
>
> #### I tried to connect to the following addresses, but was blocked by
firewall rules:
>
> - `https://api.github.com/graphql`
> - Triggering command: `/usr/bin/gh gh issue list --state open --limit
5 --json number,title,labels` (http block)
>
> If you need me to access, download, or install something from one of
these locations, you can either:
>
> - Configure [Actions setup
steps](https://gh.io/copilot/actions-setup-steps) to set up my
environment, which run before the firewall is enabled
> - Add the appropriate URLs or hosts to the custom allowlist in this
repository's [Copilot coding agent
settings](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-python-environments/settings/copilot/coding_agent)
(admins only)
>
> </details>

commit.message:
fix(datasets): silently drop degenerate `bboxes` before Alb. val (#825)

COCO annotations sometimes place a bounding box exactly on the image
boundary, resulting in x_min == x_max (or y_min == y_max) after
coordinate clipping. Albumentations' check_bboxes rejects these with
"x_max is less than or equal to x_min", crashing DataLoader workers.

Filter out zero-width/zero-height boxes in _apply_geometric_transform
before passing to Albumentations. Labels, idxs, and masks are updated
in lockstep; because idxs carries original indices, downstream
_filter_per_instance_fields still slices per-instance fields correctly
from the un-filtered target.

Adds two regression tests covering the bbox-only and bbox+masks cases.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(training): add batch_size='auto' with train-step probe  (#814)

- Add segmentation_head to _make_synthetic_batch and thread through _probe_step
  and probe_max_micro_batch so targets include 'masks' when segmentation_head=True
- Add Google-style docstrings to AutoBatchResult, probe_max_micro_batch,
  recommend_grad_accum_steps, resolve_auto_batch_config
- Use built-in dict/list/tuple; remove redundant min() with comment
- Add test_probe_step_with_real_segmentation_criterion (CUDA) to cover loss_masks path
- Use max multi-scale resolution when multi_scale=True for worst-case probe
- Add auto_batch_max_targets_per_image (default 100) and synthesize that many
  targets per image so matcher/loss memory is reflected
- Add auto_batch_ema_headroom (default 0.7) and apply when use_ema=True
- Add config validators and tests for new fields

* Apply suggestions from code review

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jburkhardt4 <189179693+jburkhardt4@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(training): rich progress bar, key metrics display, PTL warning fixes (#822)

* feat(training): rich progress bar, key metrics display, PTL warning fixes

Progress bar:
- `TrainConfig.progress_bar` changed from `bool` to `Literal["rich","tqdm"] | None`
  — explicit control over bar style; `None` disables (default)
- `build_trainer()` adds `RichProgressBar` with `metrics_format=".3e"` for
  scientific notation (fixes `train/lr: 0.000` truncation)

Metrics in progress bar:
- `train/loss` forced `on_step=True` — visible every step regardless of
  `train_log_on_step` config
- `val/loss` added `prog_bar=True`
- `train/lr_min` / `train/lr_max` removed from progress bar (clutter); still
  logged to CSV/TensorBoard
- `val/mAP_50_95`, `val/mAP_50`, `val/ema_mAP_50_95`, `val/F1` added
  `prog_bar=True` in `COCOEvalCallback`

PTL warning fixes:
- `transfer_batch_to_device` moved from `RFDETRModule` to `RFDETRDataModule`
  — silences PTL heuristic warning when a DataModule is passed to `fit()`
- `pyproject.toml`: add `notebooks/*.py` to ruff `per-file-ignores` for E402
  (cell-local imports are intentional in percent-format scripts)

---

- trainer.py: add explicit TQDMProgressBar callback for progress_bar="tqdm" so it
  no longer silently falls through to RichProgressBar on PTL 2.6
- module.py: respect train_log_on_step config for train/loss (was hard-coded on_step=True,
  inconsistent with all other train/* metrics)
- test_module.py: remove TestTransferBatchToDevice (hook moved to DataModule)
- test_datamodule.py: add TestTransferBatchToDevice (4 tests) targeting
  RFDETRDataModule.transfer_batch_to_device; add _make_batch helper + NestedTensor import
- test_trainer.py (new): TestProgressBarCallbacks (rich/tqdm/None) +
  TestCoerceLegacyProgressBar (5 parametrized legacy-bool coercion tests)
- test_detr_shim.py / __init__.py: fix stale RFDETRModule refs to RFDETRModelModule
  to complete the class rename the PR had partially applied
- Updated all imports, type hints, and assertions to reflect the class rename.
- Adjusted documentation examples to use `RFDETRModelModule`.
- Ensured compatibility across training, CLI, and inference workflows.
- Replaced deprecated references in synthetic benchmarks, COCO tests, and helper functions.
- Updated all docstrings, comments, and assertions to align with the new class name.
- Ensured consistent terminology across documentation, tests, and helper functions.
- Updated import paths across codebase and tests to reflect new module names.
- Ensured consistent references in documentation, comments, and test fixtures.
- Addressed all affected helper functions and regression tests.
- Adjusted test imports and mock paths to align with the `module` → `module_model` and `datamodule` → `module_data` renaming.
- Updated class references from `RFDETRModule` to `RFDETRModelModule` in test files.
- Corrected references from `module` to `module_model` and `datamodule` to `module_data`.
- Updated docstrings and auto-doc paths for consistency with recent renames.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(mps): eliminate MPS CPU fallbacks for `grid_sample` and bicubic backward (#821)

- `grid_sampler_2d_backward`: backward pass of `F.grid_sample` not yet
  implemented on MPS.  Replaced with `_bilinear_grid_sample` in
  `utilities/tensors.py` — gather-based index arithmetic that is native
  on every backend.  Non-MPS devices delegate to `F.grid_sample` unchanged
  (CUDA fused kernel preserved).  Affects deformable attention fallback
  (`ms_deform_attn_core_pytorch`) and segmentation point sampling
  (`point_sample`).
- `_upsample_bicubic2d_aa_backward`: anti-aliased bicubic backward not
  implemented on MPS.  Conditionally disable `antialias` on MPS in both
  DINOv2 position embedding interpolation paths (`dinov2.py`,
  `dinov2_with_windowed_attn.py`).

Adds 35 parity tests in `tests/utilities/test_tensors.py` verifying the
manual bilinear path matches `F.grid_sample` numerically (atol=1e-5) and
passes `torch.autograd.gradcheck`.

- Move padding_mode validation before shape unpack for fail-fast behaviour
- Cast bilinear weights to input.dtype to preserve float16 on MPS
- Fix mislabelled bicubic comment in dinov2.py
- Update stale point_sample docstring to reference _bilinear_grid_sample

---------

commit.message:
fix: net_r_realized includes full trade outcome + engine-specific PTP params

Two bugs in simulate_trade_outcome():

1. net_r_realized = realized_r (PTP portion only)
   Should be total_r (PTP + remaining position result)
   Effect: losses showed -0.05R (commission only) instead of -1.05R
           wins showed 0.45R (PTP) instead of actual total outcome
   Fix: replace round(realized_r,3) -> round(total_r,3) on all exit paths

2. PTP params hardcoded to engine_params['judas_swing']
   Should use signal.engine_name to get correct engine's partial_tp_rr/vol
   Effect: ict_enigma (ptp_rr=1.75) and ict_venom (ptp_rr=1.0) both used
           judas_swing defaults instead of their optimizer-tuned values
   Fix: ep = settings.engine_params.get(signal.engine_name, {})

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: 3D grid visualization, interactive 2D topology, SSE streaming

3D Command View (/grid-3d):
- Interactive 3D topology with Three.js/React Three Fiber
- Station-specific models: generator (arc reactor core, cooling tower,
  smokestack with animated steam), solar farm (20 tilted PV panels,
  inverter), load substations (HV transformers, surge arresters)
- Catenary physics transmission lines (y = a·cosh(x/a))
- Animated power flow particles (sphere-based, speed ∝ power magnitude)
- Click-to-inspect: detailed telemetry/status overlay panel
- Hover effects with subtle scale animation
- Pulsing status glow rings on foundation pads
- Blinking aviation warning lights
- Auto-start simulation on page visit

2D Dashboard Upgrades:
- Draggable topology nodes in React Flow
- Click bus node → detailed right-side inspector panel
- Click transmission line → flow info modal
- Type-specific icons (⚡ generator, ☀️ solar, 🏭 load)
- Animated edges with flow dots and MW labels
- Attack/healing pulse animations on nodes

Data Streaming:
- New SSE hook (useSSEGridData) replaces 1s polling
- Persistent EventSource connection with auto-reconnect
- Shield status now published via EventBus
- Both 2D and 3D pages use SSE

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
